intro: Olearia traversiorum, also known as Karamu, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Olearia traversiorum include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
